transcript.question: Walk me through a concrete example of your Go work, with the trade-offs you made.
transcript.answer: I led a team of four to rebuild the payments gateway in Go, cut p99 latency from 400ms to 80ms, and rolled it out to production over six weeks.

transcript.question: Walk me through a concrete example of your Postgres work, with the trade-offs you made.
transcript.answer: I designed the Postgres schema and query paths for that service, added partial indexes on the settlement table, and reduced a slow report from minutes to under ten seconds.

transcript.question: Walk me through a concrete example of your gRPC work, with the trade-offs you made.
transcript.answer: We used gRPC with protobuf for service-to-service calls and exposed a REST gateway for third-party integrations; I owned the proto design and error-status mapping.

transcript.question: Walk me through a concrete example of your Kubernetes work, with the trade-offs you made.
transcript.answer: I containerised the service and wrote Kubernetes deployments with HPA and liveness/readiness probes; we ran it on a managed cluster in staging and production.
